ANALYTICAL TEST METHOD
Method Title: Assay of Metformin Hydrochloride by HPLC
Method Number: ATM-CHEM-0089
Version: 1.0
Effective Date: 2025-02-01
Department: Analytical Chemistry / Quality Control
Author: Dr. Priya Nair
Reviewed By: Lab Manager
Approved By: QA Director

1. PURPOSE AND SCOPE

This method describes the HPLC procedure for the determination of Metformin Hydrochloride content in finished product tablets. This method applies to QC release testing and stability samples.

2. PRINCIPLE

Metformin Hydrochloride is separated by reversed-phase HPLC and quantified by UV detection at 232 nm against a reference standard of known purity.

3. SAFETY CONSIDERATIONS

- Acetonitrile is flammable and toxic by inhalation. Use in a ventilated fume hood.
- Wear appropriate PPE: laboratory coat, safety glasses, gloves.
- Dispose of organic waste per facility waste management procedures.

4. REAGENTS AND MATERIALS

4.1 Mobile Phase
   - Component A: Potassium phosphate buffer, pH 6.8
   - Component B: Acetonitrile (HPLC grade)
   - Ratio: Approximately 70:30 (A:B) — adjust as needed for acceptable peak shape

4.2 Reference Standard
   - Metformin Hydrochloride Reference Standard (source not specified)
   - Potency: Use as labeled

4.3 Equipment
   - HPLC system with UV detector
   - Analytical balance (calibrated)
   - pH meter (calibrated)
   - Volumetric glassware (Class A)

5. COLUMN AND CHROMATOGRAPHIC CONDITIONS

5.1 Column: C18, 150 × 4.6 mm, 5 µm particle size
5.2 Flow rate: 1.0 mL/min
5.3 Detection wavelength: 232 nm
5.4 Injection volume: 20 µL
5.5 Run time: 15 minutes
5.6 Column temperature: Ambient (no specification provided)

6. SYSTEM SUITABILITY

Prior to sample analysis, verify that the system suitability criteria are satisfactory:
- Theoretical plates: Should be acceptable
- Tailing factor: Should be acceptable
- %RSD of standard peak areas (n=5): Should meet acceptable criteria
- Resolution: Adequate separation from any interfering peaks

6.1 If system suitability is not met, investigate the cause before proceeding. Do not proceed with analysis if system suitability fails.

7. STANDARD PREPARATION

7.1 Weigh approximately 50 mg of Metformin HCl Reference Standard into a 100 mL volumetric flask.
7.2 Dissolve in mobile phase and dilute to volume.
7.3 Dilute 5 mL of this solution to 100 mL with mobile phase to give a final concentration of approximately 25 µg/mL.
7.4 Prepare a minimum of 5 replicate injections for system suitability.

8. SAMPLE PREPARATION

8.1 Weigh and finely powder not less than 20 tablets.
8.2 Weigh accurately a portion of powder equivalent to approximately 500 mg of Metformin HCl into a 1000 mL volumetric flask.
8.3 Add 700 mL mobile phase, sonicate for 15 minutes with occasional shaking.
8.4 Dilute to volume with mobile phase and filter through a 0.45 µm membrane filter.
8.5 Dilute 5 mL of the filtrate to 100 mL with mobile phase.

9. ANALYSIS

9.1 Inject the reference standard solutions and sample solutions in the following sequence:
   - Blank (mobile phase)
   - System suitability standard (5 injections)
   - Reference standard (3 injections)
   - Sample solutions
   - Reference standard (bracket)

9.2 Calculate the assay result using the following formula:
   % Assay = (Area_Sample / Area_Standard) × (W_Standard / W_Sample) × (P_Standard / 100) × (V_Sample / V_Standard) × 100

10. ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

10.1 Assay result: 98.0% to 102.0% of label claim (as Metformin Hydrochloride)
10.2 System suitability: As defined in Section 6 (criteria deemed acceptable by analyst)

11. CALCULATIONS

All calculations shall be performed using validated spreadsheet templates or LIMS.

12. RESULTS REPORTING

12.1 Results shall be reported to 1 decimal place.
12.2 All raw data and integration parameters shall be saved and retained per data management procedures.

13. REFERENCES

13.1 21 CFR Part 211 — CGMP for Finished Pharmaceuticals
13.2 ICH Q2(R1) — Validation of Analytical Procedures

Appendix A: Chromatogram examples (to be added upon method validation completion)
